2: But how should man be just with God?
3: He cannot answer him one of a thousand.
4: Who hath hardened himself himself against him, and hath prospered?
5: Which overturneth them in his anger.
8: And treadeth upon the waves of the sea.
9: And the chambers of the south.
11: He passeth on also, but I perceive him not.
12: Who will say unto him, What doest thou?
13: The proud helpers do stoop under him.

15: But But I would make supplication to my judge.
16: Yet Yet would I not believe that he had hearkened unto my voice.
17: And multiplieth my wounds without cause.

20: If I say If I say , I am am perfect, it shall also prove me perverse.
22: He destroyeth the perfect and the wicked.
23: He will laugh at the trial of the innocent.
24: If not, where, and and who is is he?
25: They flee away, they see no good.
26: As the eagle that that hasteth to the prey.
27: I will leave off my heaviness, and comfort myself: myself:

30: And make my hands never so clean;
31: And mine own clothes shall abhor me.
